messages.subject: Re: Running template with specific git branch doesn't work
messages.body: Some more details might help. Here is a screenshot that shows how to pick a branch on running. Keep in mind, the synchronization of the project needs to happen with the correct branch.

messages.subject: Re: Ansible Vault
messages.body:
-

Vault is 100% used for secrets and encrypted sensitive data in AAP. Here is an example playbook that uses vault variables:

--- - name: Deploy application with vault secrets hosts: webservers vars_files: - vault_secrets.yml tasks: - name: Configure database connection ansible.builtin.template: src: db_config.j2 dest: /etc/app/db.conf vars: db_password: "{{ vault_db_password }}"

The encrypted vault files are expected to be *.yaml files that are part of your project. A corresponding vault credential is provided and created with the password to unlock the vault.

messages.subject: Re: /etc/issue vs /etc/issue.net
messages.body:
-

They are usually the same file. /etc/issue is for local terminal logins while /etc/issue.net is for remote logins like Telnet/SSH.

You can verify the difference with:

diff /etc/issue /etc/issue.net cat -A /etc/issue cat -A /etc/issue.net

There are times where the messages are different as this would be a BANNER message for someone accessing the system.

messages.subject: LB0002 ILT Release - AU294 Shared Classroom
messages.body:
Instructors -

I'm happy to report that the LB0002 manifest and shared lab environment has been released to Instructor Central. It is really important to note that now almost every single system uses a cloud-init ISO image to customize the machines at boot up.

The workstation system is designed to be "disconnected" and therefore, the /etc/rht is set to venue of ILT. I was able to perform the course autotest with the lab CLI tool and it was able to successfully start/finish all lab scripts.

The manifest is available on Instructor Central.

Tagging:    for awareness.

Thanks,
Travis

messages.subject: Re: Red Hat Enterprise Linux Automation with Ansible (AU294)
messages.body:
-

Red Hat training courses are not and have never been designed to be preparation courses for an exam. The training courses cover a list of objectives and there is a clear outline.

For example, you might be creating a user with the user module in the course. However, you've also learned about variables, vault files, and specialized inventory. One could create or extend the user creation scenario:

- name: Create users from vault-encrypted variable file hosts: all vars_files: - group_vars/all/users.yml tasks: - name: Ensure user exists ansible.builtin.user: name: "{{ item.name }}" groups: "{{ item.groups }}" state: present loop: "{{ managed_users }}"

Good luck if you decide to retake the exam!

messages.subject: Re: Red Hat exam version for instructors
messages.body:
-

They have a version for RHEL9 and RHEL10 for the EX200. Both have been available. As for the EX294 exam, that is more AAP and Ansible focused versus being a RHEL focused exam.

Not sure I understand the topic you posit as Red Hat exam version for Instructors, but the exams would be the same for anyone taking them.

messages.subject: Re: Hardware and software requirements for the remote Red Hat Exam
messages.body:
-

Basically you will be downloading a live Linux ISO which will run the exam environment. There aren't any specific requirements related to the OS, but that the computer will need to be capable of running Fedora 41 essentially.

The PDF mentions things on how to create the live ISO, but Balena Etcher or Fedora Media Writer will both write it to the USB disk.

You should most likely be able to use either of your systems as those are both x86_64 compatible. I would suggest downloading the ISO and running the test.

messages.subject: Getting started with Podman and containers
messages.body:
Here is a quick reference for common Podman commands to get you started:

# Pull and run a container podman pull registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9/ubi podman run -it --rm ubi9/ubi /bin/bash # List running containers podman ps # Build from Containerfile podman build -t myapp:latest . # Push to registry podman push myapp:latest quay.io/myuser/myapp:latest

For more information, see the Podman documentation.
